title: ERA5-Land hourly data to SWAT inputs
text: case run six ERA5-Land hourly NetCDF files: processed study area 229 grid cells hourly cube 2,208 layers. expected long table created cube2table() 505,632 rows per variable. choosing extraction path, inspect downloaded files raster_info(). number layers time resolution make clear whether file hourly, daily, multi-variable cube.
code: case_root <- Sys.getenv("WCSWATIN_CASE_ROOT") input_dir <- file.path(case_root, "data/input") output_dir <- file.path(case_root, "data/output") raster_info(file.path(input_dir, "nc_files/hourly/hourly_2m_temperature_2025.nc")) raster_info(file.path(input_dir, "nc_files/daily/daily_2m_temperature_2025.nc"))

title: ERA5-Land hourly data to SWAT inputs
text: study_area_records() combines one raster model layer, basin boundary, DEM create table used extraction workflow. Precomputed result:
code: study_area_tbl <- study_area_records(   raster_model = nc_file,   roi = basin_limit,   dem = dem_raster )  data.table::fwrite(   study_area_tbl,   file.path(output_dir, "study_area.csv") )

title: ERA5-Land hourly data to SWAT inputs
text: main_input_var() creates mainFile.csv target variable. case creates main files original variables derived variables. Example mainFile.csv rows u10:
code: variables <- c("u10", "v10", "d2m", "t2m", "ssrd", "tp", "ws10", "rh2m")  for (var in variables) {   main_tbl <- main_input_var(     study_area = file.path(output_dir, "study_area.csv"),     var_name = var   )    data.table::fwrite(     main_tbl,     file.path(output_dir, var, "mainFile.csv")   ) }

title: ERA5-Land hourly data to SWAT inputs
text: cube2table() converts NetCDF cube one long table containing extracted values study area cells. future integration useful: raster layers can extracted independently, high-level cube2table() call stays . Users choose execution strategy future::plan() calling function, workflow can run sequentially, local parallel workers, another compatible future backend. benchmarked run, extracting one variable took 6.6 7.1 minutes 8-10 local workers, compared 39.5 minutes sequentially. performance gain comes changing future plan, rewriting extraction code. example, sequential useful debugging, multisession portable across operating systems, multicore can faster Unix-like systems outside GUI sessions, future backends can used user’s environment supports . See future overview backend options: https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/future/vignettes/future-1-overview.html.
code: future::plan(future::multicore, workers = 10)  cube2table(   input_path = nc_file,   n_layers = 2208,   study_area = file.path(output_dir, "study_area.csv"),   future_scheduling = 1,   final_dir = file.path(output_dir, "u10/intermediate/layer_h"),   side_effect = "only" )  future::plan(future::sequential)

what: Step 4: write one series per pixel
title: ERA5-Land hourly data to SWAT inputs
text: layervalues2pixel() transforms long layer table SWAT-style time-series files, one file per grid cell. case created 229 hourly files per original variable.
code: layervalues2pixel(   layer_values = file.path(output_dir, "u10/intermediate/layer_h/tbls.csv"),   main_tbl = file.path(output_dir, "u10/mainFile.csv"),   col_name = "20251001",   inline_output = TRUE,   path_output = file.path(output_dir, "u10/intermediate/pixel_h"),   append = FALSE )

what: Step 5: aggregate hourly values to daily values
title: ERA5-Land hourly data to SWAT inputs
text: Daily aggregation depends variable semantics. Mean aggregation: Accumulated variables use hour stores completed accumulation period. ERA5-Land daily accumulated values timestamped midnight, value_hour = 0.
code: daily_aggregation(   folder_in = file.path(output_dir, "u10/intermediate/pixel_h"),   folder_out = file.path(output_dir, "u10/pixel_d"),   pattern = ".txt$",   from = "2025-10-01 01",   to = "2025-12-31 23",   drop_first_record = FALSE,   aggregation_function = mean,   na.rm = FALSE ) daily_aggregation(   folder_in = file.path(output_dir, "tp/intermediate/pixel_h_mm"),   folder_out = file.path(output_dir, "tp/pixel_d"),   pattern = ".txt$",   from = "2025-10-01 01",   to = "2025-12-31 23",   drop_first_record = FALSE,   mode = "value_at_hour",   value_hour = 0,   na.rm = FALSE )

title: ERA5-Land hourly data to SWAT inputs
text: source product already daily, workflow can skip hourly intermediate files daily_aggregation(). case, use raster_info() confirm variable, unit, layer count, date range, run cube2table() daily cube pass result directly layervalues2pixel(). Another option reduce hourly NetCDF locally extraction. datacube_aggregation() can apply daily aggregation function, can select layer timestamped specific hour cube2table() runs. accumulated products whose daily value stored specific timestamp, use mode = "value_at_hour". ERA5-Land accumulated values timestamped 00:00 represent previous day, output dates can shifted back one day. first selected 00:00 layer belongs day requested period, drop writing daily cube.
code: daily_info <- raster_info(   file.path(input_dir, "nc_files/daily/daily_2m_temperature_2025.nc") )  cube2table(   input_path = file.path(input_dir, "nc_files/daily/daily_2m_temperature_2025.nc"),   n_layers = daily_info$n_layers,   study_area = file.path(output_dir, "study_area.csv"),   final_dir = file.path(output_dir, "t2m/intermediate/layer_d"),   side_effect = "only" )  layervalues2pixel(   layer_values = file.path(output_dir, "t2m/intermediate/layer_d/tbls.csv"),   main_tbl = file.path(output_dir, "t2m/mainFile.csv"),   col_name = "20251001",   inline_output = FALSE,   path_output = file.path(output_dir, "t2m/pixel_d"),   append = FALSE ) datacube_aggregation(   input_path = file.path(input_dir, "nc_files/hourly/hourly_2m_temperature_2025.nc"),   output_filename = file.path(output_dir, "t2m_daily_mean.tif"),   fun = mean,   cores = 4 ) datacube_aggregation(   input_path = file.path(input_dir, "nc_files/hourly/hourly_total_precipitation_2025.nc"),   output_filename = file.path(output_dir, "tp_daily_value_at_00.tif"),   mode = "value_at_hour",   value_hour = 0,   date_shift_days = -1,   drop_first_layer = TRUE )

what: Step 6: derive wind speed and relative humidity
title: ERA5-Land hourly data to SWAT inputs
text: windspeed_calculator() derives wind speed daily u10 v10. rh_calculator() derives hourly relative humidity dewpoint air temperature, followed daily mean aggregation.
code: windspeed_calculator(   folder_uas = file.path(output_dir, "u10/pixel_d"),   folder_vas = file.path(output_dir, "v10/pixel_d"),   folder_out = file.path(output_dir, "ws10/pixel_d"),   col_name = "20251001",   file_name_output = "ws10",   pattern = ".txt$" ) rh_calculator(   folder_dpt = file.path(output_dir, "d2m/intermediate/pixel_h"),   folder_tas = file.path(output_dir, "t2m/intermediate/pixel_h"),   folder_out = file.path(output_dir, "rh2m/intermediate/pixel_h"),   file_name_output = "rh2m",   m_value = 7.591386,   Tn_value = 240.7263,   pattern = ".txt$" )

title: Station interpolation workflow
text: input folder contains one precipitation file per station pcp.txt main file station metadata. case 14 stations 31 daily records. daily station table shape expected interpolation functions:
code: station_folder <- file.path(case_root, "data/input/weather-station-data/pcp_stations")  pcp_table <- files_to_table(   files_path = station_folder,   files_pattern = "p-",   start_date = "2017-03-01",   end_date = "2017-03-31",   na_value = -99,   neg_to_zero = FALSE )

title: Station interpolation workflow
text: count_na() identifies missing values fill_gap() fills station gaps using configured temporal correlation period. filled table can written back SWAT-style station files.
code: count_na(pcp_table[-1], percent = TRUE)  gap_filled <- fill_gap(   dataset = pcp_table,   corPeriod = "daily" )  count_na(gap_filled[-1], percent = TRUE) table_to_files(   table = gap_filled,   folder_path = gap_filled_output,   first_date = "2017-03-01",   file_extension = "txt" )

title: Station interpolation workflow
text: point_to_daily() changes perspective one file per station one table per day. save_daily_tbl() writes daily tables disk. precomputed run created 31 daily tables.
code: daily_tables <- point_to_daily(   my_folder = gap_filled_output,   var_pattern = "p-",   main_pattern = "pcp",   start_date = "20170301",   end_date = "20170331",   interval = "day",   na_value = -99,   neg_to_zero = TRUE,   prefix = "day_" )  save_daily_tbl(   tbl_list = daily_tables,   path = daily_tbl_output )

title: Station interpolation workflow
text: ts_to_area() applies trend-surface interpolation daily station table returns multi-layer raster. case wrote selected days GeoTIFF mapping inspection. generated GeoTIFF 2017-03-18 11.4 MB treated case output artifact.
code: raster_interpolated <- ts_to_area(   my_folder = daily_tbl_output,   bassin_limit_path = basin_limit,   poly_degree = 2,   resolution = 0.001 )  terra::writeRaster(   terra::rast(raster_interpolated)[[18]],   file.path(output_dir, "interpolation/raster_interpolated/interpolated_2017-03-18.tif"),   overwrite = TRUE )

what: Aggregate hourly files to daily files
title: Preparing weather and climate inputs with wcswatin
text: daily_aggregation() works folders SWAT-style time-series files. example creates one hourly table aggregates two daily values. accumulated variables timestamped end accumulation period, use mode = "value_at_hour". ERA5-Land-style daily accumulated values stored 00:00, keep value_hour = 0 include closing 00:00 record input period.
code: hourly_folder <- file.path(tempdir(), "wcswatin_hourly") daily_folder <- file.path(tempdir(), "wcswatin_daily") dir.create(hourly_folder, showWarnings = FALSE)  hourly_values <- data.frame(t2m = seq(280, 303, length.out = 48)) utils::write.table(   hourly_values,   file.path(hourly_folder, "t2m_20251001.txt"),   row.names = FALSE,   sep = "," )  daily_aggregation(   folder_in = hourly_folder,   folder_out = daily_folder,   from = "2025-10-01 00",   to = "2025-10-02 23",   drop_first_record = FALSE,   aggregation_function = mean ) #>   |                                                                              |                                                                      |   0%  |                                                                              |======================================================================| 100%  input_table(file.path(daily_folder, "t2m_20251001.txt")) #>         t2m #>       <num> #> 1: 285.6277 #> 2: 297.3723 accumulated_folder <- file.path(tempdir(), "wcswatin_accumulated") dir.create(accumulated_folder, showWarnings = FALSE)  accumulated_values <- data.frame(tp = seq_len(24)) utils::write.table(   accumulated_values,   file.path(accumulated_folder, "tp_20251001.txt"),   row.names = FALSE,   sep = "," )  value_hour_folder <- file.path(tempdir(), "wcswatin_value_hour")  daily_aggregation(   folder_in = accumulated_folder,   folder_out = value_hour_folder,   from = "2025-10-01 01",   to = "2025-10-02 00",   drop_first_record = FALSE,   mode = "value_at_hour",   value_hour = 0 ) #>   |                                                                              |                                                                      |   0%  |                                                                              |======================================================================| 100%  input_table(file.path(value_hour_folder, "tp_20251001.txt")) #>       tp #>    <int> #> 1:    24

text: Station files can consolidated one table quality checks simple summaries. inverse operation writes station column back separate file. point_to_daily() reorganizes station observations one table per day. commonly used spatial interpolation stations.
code: station_folder <- system.file("extdata/pcp_stations", package = "wcswatin")  pcp_table <- files_to_table(   files_path = station_folder,   files_pattern = "^p-",   start_date = "2002-01-01",   end_date = "2002-01-31",   na_value = -99,   neg_to_zero = TRUE )  pcp_table[1:5, 1:5] #>         date p-1553003 p-1554006 p-1555005 p-1556007 #> 1 2002-01-01         0       8.4         0       0.0 #> 2 2002-01-02        NA      12.3         0       0.0 #> 3 2002-01-03         0       0.0         0      30.4 #> 4 2002-01-04         0       5.1         0      38.6 #> 5 2002-01-05         0       0.0        NA       0.0 count_na(pcp_table, percent = TRUE)[1:5, ] #>      column  Prop_NA #> 1      date  0.00000 #> 2 p-1553003 22.58065 #> 3 p-1554006 12.90323 #> 4 p-1555005 12.90323 #> 5 p-1556007 16.12903 station_out <- file.path(tempdir(), "wcswatin_station_files")  table_to_files(   table = pcp_table[, -1],   folder_path = station_out,   first_date = "20020101",   file_extension = "txt" )  head(list.files(station_out)) #> [1] "p-1553003.txt" "p-1554006.txt" "p-1555005.txt" "p-1556007.txt" #> [5] "p-1653002.txt" "p-1653004.txt" daily_tables <- point_to_daily(   my_folder = station_folder,   start_date = "20020101",   end_date = "20020131" )  save_daily_tbl(   tbl_list = daily_tables,   path = file.path(tempdir(), "wcswatin_daily_station_tables") )

title: Weather and Climate Inputs for SWAT
text: wcswatin (Weather & Climate SWAT Input) open-source R package preparing gridded climate data weather station observations inputs Soil & Water Assessment Tool (SWAT). package provides two complementary workflows: Raster/NetCDF processing: inspect NetCDF GeoTIFF files, define study-area cells, extract gridded values, aggregate hourly daily data, write SWAT-style time-series files. Station data interpolation: read station files, fill gaps, reorganize daily observations, interpolate values trend surfaces, write point-based SWAT inputs. Developed funding Critical Ecosystem Partnership Fund (CEPF).

title: Weather and Climate Inputs for SWAT
text: Inspect NetCDF variables, units, extents, layer counts, time resolution raster_info() var_names() Process NetCDF GeoTIFF raster files climate data providers ERA5-Land, GPM IMERG PERSIANN Extract gridded data watershed cells reference points Support hourly workflows, daily NetCDF products, local datacube aggregation datacube_aggregation() Use future-based parallel extraction cube2table() Prepare SWAT-style weather files station interpolation outputs Fill gaps summarize station gridded time series

title: wcswatin 0.1.1
text: Minor maintenance release following CRAN pre-submission checks. Added package-specific terms inst/WORDLIST. Excluded cran-comments.md source package. Updated package metadata README citation examples version 0.1.1.

title: wcswatin 0.1.0
text: Reorganized R source files logical domain, making package structure match main workflows closely. Reorganized test suite logical domains added shared test helpers temporary files directories. Added compact NetCDF example files support runnable examples, metadata inspection, raster workflow tests. Replaced previous Makefile-based workflow justfile common development tasks.

title: wcswatin 0.1.0
text: Added raster_info() summarize raster NetCDF metadata, including variables, units, layer counts, spatial extent, CRS, time range. Added ts_point_to_files() save ts_to_point() outputs individual SWAT-style point files. Added value_at_hour support daily_aggregation() products whose daily value stored specific hour. Added value_at_hour, date_shift_days, drop_first_layer support datacube_aggregation() hourly raster cubes can reduced extraction. Improved tbl_from_references() reference points can supplied using package’s standard table, vector, path input patterns.

title: wcswatin 0.1.0
text: Standardized longitude column names LON. Renamed take_out_first_record argument daily_aggregation() drop_first_record. Renamed negatif_number argument station/table preprocessing neg_to_zero.

title: wcswatin 0.1.0
text: Added reusable input validation helpers clearer error messages across raster, table, station, interpolation workflows. Ensured variable derivation helpers create output directories consistently. Removed duplicate internal file_name() implementation. Removed vroom dependency main file-reading workflow. Expanded test coverage raster aggregation, cube extraction, metadata, reference extraction, station input, table aggregation, table /O, variable derivation, trend-surface interpolation.

title: wcswatin 0.1.0
text: Added lightweight executable vignette using bundled example data. ERA5-Land hourly data SWAT inputs; station interpolation workflow; running similar external case study. Updated README pkgdown navigation reflect current package workflows.
